steps.title: Identify the angle relationship
steps.content: Lines l and k are parallel, cut by a transversal. Angle a° is at the intersection with line l, and angle 64° is at the intersection with line k. Both angles are on the same side of the transversal but on the exterior sides of their respective parallel lines, making them co-exterior angles (also called co-related exterior or consecutive exterior angles).

steps.title: Apply the co-exterior angle theorem
steps.content: Co-exterior angles formed by a transversal crossing two parallel lines are supplementary — they sum to 180°: $$a + 64 = 180$$ $$a = 180 - 64 = 116$$

steps.title: Test all four choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '26': 26 + 64 = 90, not 180. This would be true only if a and 64° were complementary, which co-exterior angles are not. ✗
  • Choice B — '64': 64 would apply if a and 64° were corresponding or alternate angles (equal). But they are co-exterior — supplementary, not equal (since 64 ≠ 180/2 = 90). ✗
  • Choice C — '116': 116 + 64 = 180. Co-exterior angles are supplementary. ✓
  • Choice D — '154': 154 + 64 = 218 ≠ 180. ✗

steps.title: Conclusion
steps.content: Co-exterior angles with parallel lines are supplementary: a = 180 − 64 = 116. That matches choice C — C is correct.
